# Feature Rundown: <name>

## Summary

Describe the feature in a few sentences. Explain what it does, who it helps, and why it matters.

## Must Have

- List the minimum behaviors needed for the first useful version.
- Focus on what must exist for the feature to feel complete enough to build.

## Nice to Have

- List stretch ideas, polish, or follow-up improvements.
- These should be safe to postpone without blocking the core feature.

## Non-Functional Requirements

- Capture quality constraints rather than feature behavior.
- Examples: performance expectations, responsiveness, accessibility, reliability, maintainability, or import speed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- List the concrete conditions that make this feature done.
- Write these so another agent can verify whether the implementation matches the intent.

## Open Questions

- Record unresolved product or UX questions.
- Remove or answer these before finalizing implementation details.

## Related Specs

- Link the specs that define how the feature should behave technically.
- Add links as specs are created.

## Related Architecture

- Link shared architecture notes that affect this feature.
- Examples: parsing, data sources, state strategy, or persistence.
